channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Centre argues courts lack authority to classify religious practices as superstition in Sabarimala case
item.description: The Union government has told the Supreme Court that secular courts have no jurisdiction to decide whether a religious practice amounts to superstition. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta, representing th...

item.title: NDDC Champions Public-Private Partnerships to Boost Aviation and Economic Growth in Niger Delta
item.description: The Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) has renewed its call for stronger public-private partnerships (PPP) as the key to unlocking the economic potential of the Niger Delta. In a strategic meet...

item.title: Prime Minister Mark Carney and astronaut Jeremy Hansen to discuss Canada’s role in Artemis II lunar mission
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is set to speak with Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen following Artemis II, the first crewed lunar mission to include a Canadian astronaut. The conversation will highlight C...

item.title: Trump’s Iran Ceasefire Highlights Divisions Within Maga Supporters
item.description: Donald Trump's acceptance of a two-week ceasefire with Iran has revealed growing divisions within his Make America Great Again (Maga) movement. While some supporters view the pause in hostilities as a...

item.title: Trump’s Violent Rhetoric and Its Impact on American Politics
item.description: The article explores the disturbing nature of President Donald Trump's recent rhetoric, particularly regarding Iran. Known for his inflammatory language, Trump's words have escalated in their violent ...

item.title: Pakistan Mediates Potential Iran-U.S. Ceasefire
item.description: According to a recent CNN report, Pakistan’s ambassador to the United States has discussed the possibility of a ceasefire in the ongoing conflict between Iran and the United States. The discussions oc...

item.title: Acting Principal Successfully Challenges Unfair Promotion After Four Years
item.description: Bernard Solomons, a teacher who served as the acting principal at Alfons Primary School in Windmeul, Paarl, for over four years, has successfully challenged his unfair dismissal for the permanent prin...

item.title: Assessing the Hawke Government’s Impact on Australian Reform
item.description: The Hawke government (1983-1991) is often celebrated as the 'gold standard' for Australian political reform. This assessment comes from prominent figures such as Gareth Evans, who coined the term 'gol...

item.title: NBI and PNP launch joint probe into fatal Palawan shootout that killed two cops and a BuCor personnel
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) and the Philippine National Police (PNP) have started their separate investigations into a deadly shooting incident in Puerto Princesa City, Palawan that lef...

item.title: Negros Power invests ₱1.3B to modernize Bacolod and Negros Occidental electricity grid
item.description: Negros Electric and Power Corporation (Negros Power) announced a ₱1.3 billion investment aimed at modernizing its electricity distribution network in Bacolod City and nearby areas in Negros Occidental...

item.title: Israel Launches Heavy Airstrikes on Lebanon Amid Ceasefire Agreement, Hezbollah Halts Attacks
item.description: Israel conducted its heaviest airstrikes on Lebanon in weeks as tensions with Hezbollah intensified. The strikes targeted over 100 Hezbollah military sites across Beirut, the Bekaa Valley, and souther...

item.title: India Welcomes Temporary Ceasefire in West Asia Brokered by Pakistan, Expresses Hope for Peace in Ukraine
item.description: The Indian government has officially welcomed the temporary ceasefire in the West Asia region, brokered by Pakistan after six weeks of intense conflict. In a measured statement, the Ministry of Extern...

item.title: Nigerian Court Hands Down Life Sentences to Boko Haram Terrorists, Jails 14 Others
item.description: A Federal High Court in Abuja, Nigeria, done pass serious judgement for Boko Haram terrorists. Four people wey been involved in attacks from 2021 to 2024, dem don receive life imprisonment sentences. ...

item.title: White House Dismisses Iran’s Public 10-Point Peace Plan as Misleading
item.description: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt addressed the media on Wednesday, strongly criticizing several outlets for repeating what she described as a misleading narrative from Iran regarding peace...

item.title: Memorial Service for Late MP David Kiaraho to Take Place Today at Nyandarua University Grounds
item.description: A memorial service for the late David Kiaraho, the Member of Parliament for Ol Kalou, is set to be held today at the proposed Nyandarua University grounds. The service, organised by the Ad-Hoc Committ...

item.title: TMPD Chief Yolande Faro Takes the Stand at Madlanga Commission of Inquiry
item.description: On Wednesday, 8 April 2026, Tshwane Metro Police Department (TMPD) chief Yolande Faro appeared before the Madlanga commission of inquiry in Pretoria. She took the stand as part of the ongoing investig...

item.title: Analysis: Netanyahu faces political setback after US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: As Iran and the United States reach a fragile ceasefire following a month-long conflict, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu emerges as the primary political casualty according to critics. Despi...

item.title: Yanga Increase Lead at Top of Mainland Premier League with Victory over Pamba Jiji
item.description: Young Africans (Yanga) continued their dominant run in the Mainland Premier League, securing a convincing 3-0 victory over Pamba Jiji FC at CCM Kirumba Stadium on April 8, 2026. The defending champion...

item.title: Former delivery driver confesses to kidnapping and killing 7-year-old girl
item.description: A former delivery driver is under investigation after admitting to the abduction and murder of a seven-year-old girl. The child was reportedly taken from a public area and held in the suspect's van be...

item.title: Government Allocates Sh17.6 Billion to KRA for Tax Collection Boost
item.description: The Kenyan government has injected a substantial Sh17.6 billion into the Kenya Revenue Authority (KRA) as part of the Supplementary Estimates I for the 2025/2026 financial year. This move aims to stre...
